mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 100k signals stronger labor market conditions, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS rises > 100k, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production above its 3-month moving average indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where INDPRO exceeds its 3-month MA, or the signal's IC falls below 0.02 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer and business demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise > 2% MoM
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where both RSAFS and HOUST rise > 2% MoM, or the interaction effect's IC drops below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate within a 3-month window signals monetary tightening, which raises equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 60%+ of cases where FEDFUNDS rises > 20 bps, or the signal's IC remains below -0.03 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5% indicates a tight labor market, which drives corporate pricing power and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where UNRATE falls below 5%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.04 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 1.5% combined with rising GDP (GDPC1) indicates disinflationary growth, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where CPIAUCSL < 1.5% and GDPC1 rises > 1% MoM, or the interaction effect's IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month low combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary risks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO < 12-month low and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% MoM
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 50%+ of cases where INDPRO < 12-month low and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% MoM, or the interaction effect's IC drops below -0.03.
